title: Nominalisation
explanation:
Nominalisation

Nominalisation turns verbs or adjectives into nouns to make writing more formal and concise.

  • decide → decision
  • develop → development
  • aware → awareness
  • fail → failure

questions.text: There has been rapid [*development|develop|developed] in renewable energy.
The campaign aims to raise public [*awareness|aware|awaring] of mental health.
His [*failure|fail|failed] to reply cost him the contract.
We need a full [*explanation|explain|explanatory] of the new policy.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The ____ of the new law took several months. | *introduction | introduce | introduced
There was widespread ____ after the announcement. | *confusion | confuse | confused
Her ____ to the team was immediate. | *contribution | contribute | contributing
The report highlights the ____ of better training. | *importance | important | importantly
The company's [*expansion|expand|expanded] into Asia was successful.
There is growing [*concern|concerned|concerning] about data security.
The judge questioned the [*accuracy|accurate|accurately] of the statement.
We discussed the [*possibility|possible|possibly] of remote work.
